subject: Add subproject support for golang verify job
commit_message:
Add subproject support for golang verify job

A descendant 'verify-golang' job is created using
yaml anchors for common job boilerplate.

Build minion gets changed to a Docker based one
as some projects depend on it on build time.

Build script is displaced so that it supports JJB
variable interpolation.

Change-Id: I0af4235287b58a251e399a90703ac3ffffc07338
Issue-ID: INT-1498
Signed-off-by: Bartek Grzybowski <b.grzybowski@partner.samsung.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

It's tested on sandbox:

https://jenkins.onap.org/sandbox/job/integration-master-test-security-k8s-verify-golang/3/
https://jenkins.onap.org/sandbox/job/integration-master-test-security-sslendpoints-verify-golang/2/

The second one is ok, it just fails due to the lack of "build" targed in makefile, it'll be addressed in integration project.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/ci-management-jjb-verify/4474/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/ci-management-jjb-verify/4474

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> (1 comment)

I'm afraid there might be misunderstanding - there are two options for building these projects:

1. Using Docker environment:
- convenient if user has no Go environment set up
- depends only on Docker engine (common on development machines among ONAP community)
- commands to set it up might get complex: reason to use Makefile #1 (command aliases)
- caching named Docker images can increase development pace: reason to use Makefile #2 (target dependencies)

2. Using local Go environment
- usually common among various Go projects
- uses Go caching mechanisms
- no isolation engine (e.g. Docker) overhead
- short commands with optional arguments (e.g. "go build", "go test")

Makefile targets in sslendpoints for local Go environment would be only command aliases mapping  e.g. "make build" to "go build" or "make test" to "go test" (like https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/+/104229) so they were intentionally omitted.

Summing up:
1. using Docker for building project is possible but not required (Makefile provided for convenience)
2. using local Go environment is not mentioned in Makefile, but also possible as described in corresponding README sections: Prerequisites/Build (local), Building/Command (local), Running/Command (local) and Testing (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=integration.git;hb=refs%2Fchanges%2F86%2F103686%2F4;f=test%2Fsecurity%2Fsslendpoints%2FREADME)
Change has been successfully merged by Jessica Wagantall
